_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to work together to discuss and reach agreement on issues that affect our classroom.

_source.w: We are learning to work together to discuss and reach agreement on issues that affect our classroom.
y: Building consensus teaches us how to listen, compromise, and find solutions together.
Practising deliberation in class prepares us for democratic decision-making in life.
Working through disagreements respectfully makes our classroom a fairer place.
eg: Discussing as a class what makes a good decision and agreeing on criteria.
Reaching consensus on new classroom rules through respectful debate.
Deliberating about whether a rule is fair and hearing different viewpoints.
Using a 'talking stick' so everyone gets a turn to share their opinion.
Voting on a class issue after listening to all sides of the argument.
Reflecting on how the discussion process felt and what made it work well.
